name: PAN Foundation Copay Assistance
eligibility: US residents with insurance who cannot afford out-of-pocket costs for specific diseases. Income must be ≤500% FPL.
description: Covers copays, coinsurance, and deductibles for patients with chronic and life-threatening diseases across 70+ disease funds.

name: HealthWell Foundation
eligibility: US residents who are underinsured and cannot afford medications. Income-based qualification varies by disease fund.
description: Financial assistance for prescription copays, premiums, and other out-of-pocket healthcare costs for underinsured Americans.

name: The Assistance Fund
eligibility: US patients with high out-of-pocket costs for specific chronic diseases. Must have commercial insurance.
description: Helps patients with high out-of-pocket costs for specific chronic and rare diseases by providing financial assistance for treatment.

name: NORD RareCare Patient Assistance
eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with a rare disease in the US. Income-based qualification required.
description: Financial assistance for copayments, travel to specialists, and medical costs for rare disease patients.

name: Chive Charities Grant
eligibility: Individuals with rare medical diagnoses, first responders, and veterans facing medical challenges in the US.
description: Provides grants for medical equipment, treatment costs, and life-changing items for individuals with rare medical conditions.

name: MDPH — PCH Disability Compensation
eligibility: Residents of France under 60 with a permanent disability affecting daily activities. Assessed by MDPH committee.
description: Prestation de Compensation du Handicap (PCH) covers human assistance, technical aids, housing modifications, transport, and animal assistance.

name: Erasmus+ Health Mobility Grants
eligibility: Healthcare professionals and students from EU/EEA countries seeking medical training or mobility in another EU country.
description: Funds cross-border mobility for healthcare professionals and medical students for training, education, and professional development.

name: Federal Ministry of Health Research Grants
eligibility: Researchers, healthcare institutions, and organizations in Germany working on public health improvement projects.
description: Funds health research projects, disease prevention programs, and healthcare system improvement initiatives in Germany.

name: NHS Individual Funding Request (IFR)
eligibility: NHS patients in England who need a treatment not routinely commissioned. Must demonstrate clinical exceptionality.
description: Funding for treatments not normally available on the NHS when a patient can demonstrate exceptional clinical circumstances.

name: State Health Programs
organization: LEPL — Social Service Agency of Georgia
eligibility: Citizens of Georgia registered in the unified database. Priority for children, disabled individuals, and low-income families.
description: Government-funded healthcare programs covering surgeries, oncology treatment, dialysis, and essential medications for eligible citizens.

name: Child Neurology Foundation Family Support
eligibility: Families of children with neurological disorders in the US who need financial assistance for care-related expenses.
description: Grants to help families cover costs related to caring for a child with a neurological condition, including therapy and equipment.

name: MDA Care & Support Grants
eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with neuromuscular diseases covered by MDA. Must be registered with MDA.
description: Financial assistance for medical equipment repairs, vehicle modifications, and other support needs for neuromuscular disease patients.

name: CAF — AEEH (Disabled Child Allowance)
eligibility: Families in France with a child under 20 who has a disability rate of ≥80% (or ≥50% with special education needs).
description: Monthly allowance to help families cover the costs of education and care for a disabled child, with supplements based on severity.

name: CAMSP Early Intervention
eligibility: Children aged 0–6 in France with developmental delays or disabilities. No income requirement.
description: Free early intervention services including physiotherapy, speech therapy, psychomotor therapy, and psychological support for young children.

name: Shriners Children's
eligibility: Children under 18 with orthopedic conditions, burns, spinal cord injuries, or cleft lip/palate. No insurance required.
description: Free pediatric specialty care including orthopedic rehabilitation, burn care, spinal cord injury treatment, and cleft lip/palate repair.

name: Global Genes RARE Impact Grant
eligibility: US-based rare disease patient advocacy organizations. Must be a 501(c)(3) nonprofit.
description: Grants to rare disease organizations for patient programs, research advancement, awareness campaigns, and community building.

name: EURORDIS Rare Disease Support
eligibility: Rare disease patient organizations in EU/EEA countries. Individual patients can access resources and referral networks.
description: Pan-European alliance supporting rare disease patients through advocacy, research facilitation, and cross-border healthcare access.

name: Orphan Disease Center Grants
organization: Orphan Disease Center — University of Pennsylvania
eligibility: Researchers studying rare diseases. Must partner with a rare disease foundation. Open to US institutions.
description: Research grants for rare disease studies, awarded in partnership with patient foundations to accelerate therapy development.

name: RTW Foundation Ultra-Rare Grants
eligibility: Patient organizations and researchers focused on ultra-rare diseases (affecting fewer than 2,000 patients in the US).
description: Funding for ultra-rare disease research, natural history studies, and therapy development in conditions with very small patient populations.

name: FDA Orphan Products Grants Program
eligibility: Clinical researchers conducting trials for rare disease treatments, diagnostics, or devices. Must have orphan drug designation.
description: Federal grants supporting clinical development of products for rare diseases, including drugs, biologics, medical devices, and foods.

name: St. Baldrick's Foundation Research Grants
eligibility: Researchers at academic institutions conducting pediatric cancer research. Various grant types for different career stages.
description: Funds childhood cancer research including fellowships, research grants, and scholar awards to find cures for pediatric cancers.

name: Alex's Lemonade Stand Foundation
eligibility: Researchers at academic or research institutions focused on pediatric oncology. Travel grants also available for families.
description: Research grants and family support for childhood cancer, including travel assistance for families whose children are receiving treatment.

name: Courageous Parents Network Support
eligibility: Parents and caregivers of children with serious illness. No geographic or income restrictions for online resources.
description: Peer support, guidance films, and resources for families navigating a child's serious illness, including palliative and end-of-life care.

name: Children's Flight of Hope
eligibility: Children in the US who need to travel for medical treatment but cannot afford commercial air travel.
description: Free commercial airline tickets for children who need to travel to distant hospitals for specialized medical treatment.

name: Angel Flight America
eligibility: Patients (including children) in the US who need non-emergency medical air transport and cannot afford it.
description: Volunteer pilots provide free flights for patients who need to travel for medical treatment, connecting families to distant specialists.

name: GITA — Innovation Grants
eligibility: Georgian startups and SMEs with innovative technology products. Must be registered in Georgia.
description: Grants for technology startups in Georgia covering product development, prototyping, and market entry for innovative solutions.

name: NSF SBIR/STTR — Digital Health
eligibility: US small businesses with innovative digital health or biomedical technology. Must be for-profit, ≤500 employees.
description: Federal seed funding for early-stage digital health startups developing innovative technologies for healthcare improvement.

name: EIC Accelerator
eligibility: Single SMEs or startups from EU/Horizon Europe associated countries with breakthrough innovations ready to scale.
description: EU's flagship program supporting high-risk, high-impact startups with blended finance (grant + equity) to scale breakthrough innovations.

name: Horizon Europe — Health Cluster
eligibility: Consortia of at least 3 entities from 3 different EU/associated countries. Health research and innovation focus.
description: EU research and innovation funding for health projects including disease prevention, treatment optimization, and health system improvement.

name: Enterprise Georgia — Startup Funding
eligibility: Georgian entrepreneurs and early-stage startups. Must present a viable business plan. Priority for youth and women entrepreneurs.
description: Government-backed startup funding program supporting entrepreneurship in Georgia through grants, co-financing, and business development services.
